principle: Room-scale floor, ceiling, and wall identity comes from Hapax-generated geometry, compositor/drift material contracts, and world lighting; DarkPlaces BSP supplies sealed walkable surfaces, collision, occlusion, and final spatial rendering without Quake-native visible material identity.

information_surface_contract.principle: Textures are permitted only when they operate as information surfaces. They may carry orientation, state, rhythm, provenance, liveness, or homage grammar; they may not simulate identifiable scenic materials such as stone, wood, metal, brick, ceiling tile, or Quake-native texture identity.
failure_predicates: A room texture can be named as a real-world material before it can be named as an information role.
A texture repeats at a scale that reads as decorative tiling rather than spatial encoding.
A surface contains copied homage/source pixels instead of clean-room procedural homage grammar.
A texture has no declared relationship to ward placement, path orientation, source state, or drift/compositor behavior.
Texture detail competes with media legibility from the intended inspection station.
Floor, ceiling, or wall identity is carried by a filled texture instead of generated world geometry, sparse stipple, and light.

surfaces.purpose: walkable collision shell rendered as visual absence; floor legibility comes only from generated hex/stipple brush geometry and live world light, never from a filled texture pattern

hybrid_contract.quake_binding: BSP floor brush texture skip as a hidden collision carrier only
hybrid_contract.producer_binding: Hapax generates floor-facing hex lines and sparse stipple as explicit brush geometry aligned to the BSP; the WAD handle carries no pattern
hybrid_contract.memory_format: Quake WAD indexed carrier for BSP compile only; visible floor identity is generated geometry plus light
hybrid_contract.update_semantics: Static hidden shell with dynamic state carried by in-world lights and geometry-bound drift marks
hybrid_contract.aspect_policy: room-scale geometry, not media aspect; filled floor texture, wallpaper, and broad color panels are forbidden
hybrid_contract.compositor_role: Hapax owns floor geometry grammar and drift semantics; DarkPlaces owns walkable collision and spatial lighting

surfaces.purpose: overhead occlusion shell rendered as visual absence; ceiling legibility comes only from generated hex/stipple brush geometry and live world light, never from a filled texture pattern

hybrid_contract.quake_binding: BSP ceiling brush texture skip as a hidden occlusion carrier only
hybrid_contract.producer_binding: Hapax generates ceiling-facing hex lines and sparse stipple as explicit brush geometry aligned to the BSP; the WAD handle carries no pattern
hybrid_contract.memory_format: Quake WAD indexed carrier for BSP compile only; visible ceiling identity is generated geometry plus light
hybrid_contract.update_semantics: Static hidden shell with dynamic state carried by in-world lights and geometry-bound drift marks
hybrid_contract.aspect_policy: room-scale geometry, not media aspect; filled ceiling texture, wallpaper, and broad color panels are forbidden
hybrid_contract.compositor_role: Hapax owns ceiling geometry grammar and drift semantics; DarkPlaces owns occlusion and spatial lighting

surfaces.purpose: neutral no-front enclosure rendered as visual absence; wall legibility comes only from generated beam-grid/stipple brush geometry and live world light, never from a filled texture pattern

hybrid_contract.quake_binding: BSP wall brush texture skip as a hidden sealing carrier only
hybrid_contract.producer_binding: Hapax generates wall-facing beam-grid lines and sparse stipple as explicit brush geometry aligned to the BSP; the WAD handle carries no pattern
hybrid_contract.memory_format: Quake WAD indexed carrier for BSP compile only; visible wall identity is generated geometry plus live light
hybrid_contract.update_semantics: Static hidden shell with dynamic state carried by in-world lights and geometry-bound drift marks
hybrid_contract.aspect_policy: room-scale geometry, not media aspect; filled wall texture, wallpaper, and broad color panels are forbidden
hybrid_contract.compositor_role: Hapax owns wall geometry grammar and drift semantics; DarkPlaces owns room sealing and spatial lighting
